generatedBy: /audit skill v2 · Session 74 infrastructure rubric + app release/security lens
summary: Premise-verified Session 74 audit: secure caller identity and telemetry integrity, replace phantom health gates, make release/build surfaces honest, and harden bounded runtime/resource contracts without fabricating live evidence.

sample.method: three in-process read-only audit lanes plus direct live verification
sample.scanned: live src/server, client, scripts, workflows, container/nginx/public surfaces
sample.baseline: lint 0; tsc 0; Vite build 0 with verified warnings; GitHub main CI green; sitemap 10/10; sanitization clean

evidenceBoundary: Local tests cannot satisfy real human alpha, revenue, DNS, email-delivery, Obelisk RP, staging parity, deployed-header, Core Web Vitals, or visual theme evidence.
Registry and local project status agree on public-unlaunched/game, but registry cost metadata remains internally contradictory.
No sibling repo tree will be edited; registry/protocol fixes travel as Ark cargo.
AGPL upstream obligations and VaultSpark-original rights posture remain unchanged.

items.title: Make every progression, clan, tournament, and private-lobby mutation server-authoritative
items.why: Multiple Worker routes trust caller-supplied persistentId/founderId/createdBy values, and any holder of a private game ID can start the lobby.
items.recipe: Centralize verified bearer actor extraction; remove caller identity from mutation trust; enforce owner/admin roles for clan-war and tournament lifecycle; authenticate private-lobby start against creatorPersistentID; update clients and add negative authorization tests.

L1.recipe: Protect the highest-risk tournament, clan, reward, and lobby-start routes.

L2.recipe: Protect every identified mutation route, update clients, and test 401/403/owner paths.

L3.recipe: Derive all rewards from signed server match outcomes and persist a full authorization audit ledger.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for server-authoritative-mutation-boundary.

items.title: Make A/B evidence assignment-owned, unit-weighted, and replay-safe
items.why: Dock, recap, and runtime event routes accept client-selected variants and values up to 10,000, invalidating experiment decisions.
items.recipe: Use server assignments only; require value=1 and verified decision-grade identity; add eventId idempotency and bounded rejection counters; expose integrity posture in admin summaries; extract a pure tested ledger from Worker.

L1.recipe: Ignore variant hints and force unit weights.

L2.recipe: Add verified identity, event-id dedupe, rejection telemetry, pure ledger tests, and client updates.

L3.recipe: Persist signed experiment events in a shared append-only store.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for decision-grade-experiment-integrity.

items.title: Bound WebSocket payload, connection, and slow-consumer memory
items.why: Three noServer WebSocket servers inherit a 100 MiB maxPayload and spectator fan-out has no bufferedAmount ceiling.
items.recipe: Set explicit payload ceilings; enforce upgrade origin/IP budgets and handshake timeouts; cap spectator membership; close slow consumers above a buffer watermark; export rejection counters and adversarial tests.

L1.recipe: Set maxPayload and close slow spectator consumers.

L2.recipe: Add payload, upgrade-rate, connection, buffer, and spectator budgets with tests.

L3.recipe: Add distributed connection admission and per-origin adaptive budgets.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for websocket-ingress-backpressure-budget.

items.title: Derive worker readiness from live IPC, HTTP, and game-loop watermarks
items.why: Worker readiness currently passes healthy:true while experiment and alpha evidence are process-local and volatile.
items.recipe: Track master IPC age, HTTP listening, process connectivity, and game-loop tick age; return 503 on serving-dependency failure; attach observedAt/scope/watermarks; warn that process-local evidence cannot satisfy durable launch proof.

L1.recipe: Replace hardcoded healthy with server/IPC state.

L2.recipe: Add typed health snapshot, watermarks, 503 behavior, evidence scope, and stale-path tests.

L3.recipe: Add DB/exporter probes and signed multi-worker aggregation watermarks.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for worker-health-scope-watermark.

items.title: Replace the zero-exit doctor stub with a real project health contract
items.why: scripts/ops.mjs doctor prints a context-meter hint and exits 0 without tests, probes, or blockingFailing.
items.recipe: Run named public-safe probes through safe-spawn; capture source, observedAt, real exit codes, checks, warnings, and blockingFailing; support JSON/update-json; exit nonzero on blockers; regression-test a forced failing probe.

L1.recipe: Make the stub fail honestly and delegate when Studio Ops is present.

L2.recipe: Implement a self-contained project doctor plus bounded canonical delegation and failure fixtures.

L3.recipe: Add signed doctor attestations and freshness contracts for every probe.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for truthful-project-doctor.

items.title: Drive the genius list and closeout hint from the latest audit schema
items.why: The generator reads an obsolete hardcoded June audit and the closeout renderer reads cache.list.ranked while the generator writes top-level items.
items.recipe: Add one versioned cache parser; ingest newest audit JSON pending/deferred items plus task evidence; select the first live unblocked item; reject unknown schemas; integration-test generator to closeout renderer.

L1.recipe: Support both cache shapes in closeout.

L2.recipe: Unify parser, dynamic audit ingestion, ranking provenance, and integration tests.

L3.recipe: Add live premise revalidation and freshness reasons for every candidate.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for audit-driven-genius-contract.

items.title: Make headers, public CTAs, container artifacts, and promotion provenance agree
items.why: NXDOMAIN origins are advertised as playable, Nginx child add_header blocks drop CSP, Docker omits public launch files, and promote compares a version tag to a Git SHA.
items.recipe: Apply one complete security-header policy at every response location; disable dead-runtime CTAs and mark endpoints unavailable; copy/smoke public files in the image; expose build metadata and verify Git SHA/tag/digest coherently in promotion.

L1.recipe: Fix header inheritance and dead CTA truth.

L2.recipe: Also repair container public parity and promotion build-metadata verification with tests.

L3.recipe: Deploy staging, verify live headers/parity, then register canonical origins.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for release-truth-pipeline.

items.title: Eliminate production-build warning drift at the source
items.why: The production build emits unset canonical/OG placeholders, inconsistent JSON import attributes, a circular manual-chunk dependency, and misleading chunk warnings.
items.recipe: Provide public-safe metadata defaults; normalize JSON import attributes; replace circular manual chunk rules with cycle-free boundaries; set warning budgets from measured transfer truth; add a build-output warning ratchet.

L1.recipe: Fix metadata and JSON attribute warnings.

L2.recipe: Also remove circular chunking and enforce a zero-known-warning build ratchet.

L3.recipe: Route-split game UI and preload only critical entry dependencies.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for warning-free-build-boundary.

items.title: Budget what users and telemetry systems actually pay
items.why: Metrics attach unbounded game IDs while the bundle gate checks chunks independently and ignores aggregate initial JS and giant media.
items.recipe: Remove unique IDs from metric labels and enforce an attribute allowlist; parse the built entry graph for aggregate gzip/brotli budgets; add media budgets and shrink-only baselines; test high-cardinality and aggregate regressions.

L1.recipe: Remove game.id labels and sum current entry chunks.

L2.recipe: Add tested cardinality allowlist, entry/media budgets, and shrink-only baselines.

L3.recipe: Add real-user transfer telemetry and adaptive route budgets.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for observability-cardinality-transfer-budget.

items.title: Block contradictory public-audience and exempt-internal release metadata
items.why: The registry says public-unlaunched but freeTierCostStatus and audit note say exempt-internal, causing both cost gates to ALLOW falsely.
items.recipe: Add a local contradiction probe and doctor gate; keep readiness cost evidence source-labeled; ship signed Ark correction cargo for Studio Ops to change registry cost status/note; never edit the sibling tree.

L1.recipe: Detect and report the contradiction locally.

L2.recipe: Gate doctor/readiness, test it, and ship signed correction cargo.

L3.recipe: Make Studio release gates reject every audience/status contradiction centrally.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for release-cost-truth-reconciliation.

items.title: Reserve remote-AI budget only for validated provider-bound work
items.why: Several routes reserve process-local budget before identity, validation, or cache lookup, and readiness labels the per-process ceiling as a global hard cap.
items.recipe: Authenticate, validate, and check cache before reservation; reserve immediately before the SDK call; label scope as per-process; expose rejected/reserved/provider-call counters; add ordering and restart-scope tests.

L1.recipe: Correct the scope label and move reservations after validation/cache.

L2.recipe: Repair every route ordering and add counters plus tests.

L3.recipe: Back the cap with an atomic shared Studio gateway bucket.

executionLog.note: Session 74 implemented and verified the full L2 recipe for remote-ai-reservation-truth.

items.title: Replace forgeable process-global Alpha Gate counters with session-scoped evidence
items.why: The unauthenticated POST accepts caller-selected weights and synthetic events can turn the gate green.
items.recipe: Allowlist value=1 events; add evidenceSessionId, privacy-safe actor key, source labels, event-id dedupe, unique-session denominators, rate limits, and tests; test/agent samples must never impersonate human launch evidence.

L1.recipe: Clamp/allowlist/rate-limit events.

L2.recipe: Add sessions, dedupe, source labels, unique-session rates, and tests.

L3.recipe: Persist signed evidence batches and render an auditable gate ledger.

executionLog.note: Shipped authenticated, pseudonymous, deduplicated, source-labeled session evidence; only distinct human sessions can satisfy the human gate.

items.title: Make replay integrity an enforced invariant instead of an unused signature
items.why: All replay/highlight/clip reads bypass verification and production can use a public development HMAC key.
items.recipe: Centralize verified loads, fail closed outside dev/test when the secret is absent, reject tampering at all consumers, add key posture to readiness, and test tamper/missing-secret behavior.

L1.recipe: Verify all read routes.

L2.recipe: Centralize verified loads, fail closed, and test tampering.

L3.recipe: Add key-version metadata and rotation.

executionLog.note: Shipped fail-closed replay verification across all consumers, with explicit development/test exception behavior and tamper tests.

items.title: Turn the false Rematch sent state into a real cloned-config lobby corridor
items.why: The client ignores failure/URL and the server stores intent without creating a lobby; telemetry counts clicks that may do nothing.
items.recipe: Authenticate the caller, clone the ended game's safe config into a worker-local private lobby, return a standard join URL, expose failure/success in WinModal, count retention only after success, and test click→API→lobby→URL.

L1.recipe: Make failure honest and use the URL.

L2.recipe: Create and test the cloned-config corridor.

L3.recipe: Add opponent presence, expiry UX, and party carryover.

executionLog.note: Shipped authenticated rematch creation into a private sanitized lobby; UI retention is recorded only after server success.

items.title: Braid the branded convoy loop into a decisive parallel victory path
items.why: Vault mechanics are substantive, but victory still belongs entirely to inherited territory/time rules.
items.recipe: Accumulate bounded Vault Pressure from deliveries, telegraph it, open a short Breach Window at threshold, let one further objective secure a Vault Breach victory, preserve territory victory, and add lifecycle/balance/UI tests.

L1.recipe: Expose pressure telemetry only.

L2.recipe: Implement pressure→breach→victory and UI.

L3.recipe: Add team aggregation, counterplay, replay markers, and simulation.

executionLog.note: Shipped the deterministic three-delivery Vault Pressure breach window, expiry rollback, decisive next-delivery win, HUD, and telemetry.

items.title: Restore green CI with an explicit coverage ratchet
items.why: Blank E2E_BASE_URL invalidates every relative navigation; formatting and a disconnected 70% threshold keep main red despite local unit green.
items.recipe: Treat blank URL as absent, start/probe local E2E, format reported files, emit JSON coverage, enforce a checked-in no-regression baseline plus stricter critical-module floors, and test the ratchet.

L1.recipe: Fix E2E URL and formatting.

L2.recipe: Add measurable global/critical coverage ratchets.

L3.recipe: Add changed-file coverage and flake telemetry.

executionLog.note: Shipped coverage and formatting shrink-only ratchets, robust local E2E fallback, dependency-free per-chunk bundle budgets, and CI wiring.

items.title: Make the coach instant and cost-neutral by default
items.why: Known tactical triggers currently require an anonymous paid call and disappear without an API key despite sufficient structured game state.
items.recipe: Create a tested local tactical-hint policy for each trigger, render it immediately, optionally request a bounded remote rewrite only when policy enables it, preserve cooldown/cache semantics, and measure avoided calls.

L1.recipe: Fallback locally on failure.

L2.recipe: Local-first policy, bounded rewrite, telemetry, tests.

L3.recipe: Counterfactual hint evaluation from outcomes.

executionLog.note: Shipped seven deterministic local coaching triggers with bounded, opt-in remote enhancement.

items.title: Reconcile the public-unlaunched profile and make remote AI spend impossible by default
items.why: Registry says public-unlaunched while local status claims internal/exempt; thirteen model call sites lack a unified public cost contract.
items.recipe: Adopt registry audience locally, retain game type and ship Ark correction cargo, centralize remote-AI policy defaulting off, expose policy in readiness, assign only an evidence-backed free-tier status, and test contradictions.

L1.recipe: Record conflict and default remote AI off.

L2.recipe: Central policy, readiness evidence, metadata, Ark cargo.

L3.recipe: Persistent per-identity quotas after Obelisk.

executionLog.note: Corrected local public-unlaunched posture, shipped registry correction cargo, and enforced default-off attributed hourly remote-AI budgets.

items.title: Separate server health, declared checks, and release readiness
items.why: The API says ready when only the process is healthy, hardcodes passes, and cites a rights document absent from the public index.
items.recipe: Add serverStatus/releaseStatus, distinguish declared from verified evidence, derive warning/block states from live inputs, cite LICENSE/LICENSING, and update tests/UI.

L1.recipe: Correct rights and add releaseStatus.

L2.recipe: Model declared/verified evidence and derive states.

L3.recipe: Add signed build provenance.

executionLog.note: Shipped declared-versus-verified evidence, server and release statuses, explicit residual gates, and machine-readable warnings/blockers.

items.title: Make recovery and Canon wave checks machine-honest
items.why: Startup misses Session N headings, CANON-044 emits '}', task parsing ignores live bullets, and stash provenance required manual reconstruction.
items.recipe: Add recovery-provenance classification; repair CANON-044 diagnostics; support/migrate the public task schema; add prompts/initiate.md; restore audit renderer helpers from the internal registry; cover all probes.

L1.recipe: Fix CANON-044 and prompt split.

L2.recipe: Add provenance/task compatibility/renderer/tests.

L3.recipe: Auto-render a non-mutating recovery ledger.

executionLog.note: Repaired prompts, added initiate, enforced recovery provenance and task-board structure, and added audit sidecar rendering/tests.

items.title: Remove all fix-available critical/high dependency advisories
items.why: The lockfile has 34 advisories including 5 critical and 8 high across direct runtime/tooling packages.
items.recipe: Resolve exact fixed versions, package-trust every direct target, update compatible groups, then supply-chain scan, audit, typecheck, unit, coverage, build, and E2E; stop on BLOCK.

L1.recipe: Patch direct non-major fixes.

L2.recipe: Clear critical/high with full gates.

L3.recipe: Also clear fixable moderates and enforce an advisory budget.

executionLog.note: Trust-gated compatible dependency updates reduced npm audit from 34 findings to zero; Studio supply-chain scan found no incident matches.

items.title: Replace unwired progression shells with one idempotent match-outcome fan-out
items.why: Achievement/season recorders lack production callers, three components are unmounted, identity is missing in one activity path, and style schemas drift.
items.recipe: Create a server-authoritative final outcome envelope, idempotently fan into existing stores/Elo, fix identity/schema drift, mount one Progression Debrief, and test replay-safe idempotency.

L1.recipe: Fix identity/schema and wire one outcome.

L2.recipe: Fan out stores and mount consolidated UI.

L3.recipe: Persist an event ledger with rebuild support.

executionLog.note: Shipped idempotent authoritative outcome processing, Elo/history, season and achievement progression, plus the mounted debrief surface.

items.title: Build the local public-launch foundation without pretending staging exists
items.why: Sitemap compliance is 0/10; footer/legal contacts are upstream, and contact/agent/sitemap/footer-manifest surfaces are absent.
items.recipe: Add sitemap, robots, agents.json, llms.txt, JSON-LD, contact/privacy/terms surfaces, exact Studio footer/legal wording while preserving upstream attribution, and footer completeness tests; keep staging/theme/Obelisk/approval gates red.

L1.recipe: Correct footer/legal and agent files.

L2.recipe: Complete local sitemap/contact/footer foundations/tests.

L3.recipe: Add staging screenshots/CWV only after staging exists.

executionLog.note: Shipped public sitemap, legal/contact/docs/AI-agent surfaces, footer/JSON-LD/upstream attribution, and achieved local sitemap 10/10.

skipped.reason: Verified high-value architecture, but a shared PostgreSQL migration/backfill/retention rollout is external-state work; this arc will make process scope explicit and keep launch unready rather than ship a partial ledger.
All declared runtime origins are NXDOMAIN and there is no successful runtime deploy evidence. Local staging is the registered gate; no production/SPARKED flip is authorized.
Credential is READY, but no live staging origin exists to register and the generated React stub is incompatible with the Lit app. Keep quarantine and ship RP cargo only after origin exists.
Credential is READY, but the project domain is NXDOMAIN; delivery cannot be verified without DNS. Public copy stays explicitly unverified.
No automation, synthetic event, or agent action can substitute for real human behavior or a real checkout/supporter event.
Coverage ratchet exists and passes, but enabling all:true across a 3,760-line Worker before extraction would create an unusable gate. The selected security extraction/tests are the root-first step.
Base-image/cloudflared digest and signature pinning requires a separate trust-gated artifact selection pass; container parity is selected now, mutable artifact pinning is retained as explicit debt.
Tracked binary and missing Go CI are verified debt but rank below exploitable mutation/WebSocket/release-truth defects in this infrastructure pass.

notes: Public launch remains NO-GO. New pending items are the complete Session 74 implementation contract; prior shipped S73 items are retained for merge-mode provenance.
